00001 /*
00002  * lseek_syscall.c
00003  *
00004  *  Created on: May 27, 2010
00005  *      Author: Luigi Grimaudo  153740
00006  *              Piera Gueli     160119
00007  */
00008 
00009 #include <types.h>
00010 #include <kern/errno.h>
00011 #include <kern/reboot.h>
00012 #include <kern/unistd.h>
00013 #include <lib.h>
00014 #include <spl.h>
00015 #include <clock.h>
00016 #include <thread.h>
00017 #include <current.h>
00018 #include <synch.h>
00019 #include <vm.h>
00020 #include <mainbus.h>
00021 #include <vfs.h>
00022 #include <vnode.h>
00023 #include <device.h>
00024 #include <syscall.h>
00025 #include <kern/seek.h>
00026 #include <kern/fcntl.h>
00027 #include <kern/file.h>
00028 
00029 /*
00030 * lseek alters the current seek position of the file handle filehandle, seeking to a new position based on pos and whence.
00031 * If whence is
00032 * - SEEK_SET, the new position is pos.
00033 * - SEEK_CUR, the new position is the current position plus pos.
00034 * - SEEK_END, the new position is the position of end-of-file plus pos.
00035 * - anything else, lseek fails. 
00036 * Note that pos is a signed quantity.
00037 * It is not meaningful to seek on certain objects (such as the console device). All seeks on these objects fail.
00038 * Seek positions less than zero are invalid. Seek positions beyond EOF are legal.
00039 * Note that each distinct open of a file should have an independent seek pointer. 
00040 */
00041 
00042 off_t sys_lseek(int fd, off_t pos,int whence,int *retval)
00043 {
00044 
00045         struct file* fcb;
00046         struct uio u;
00047         struct iovec iov;
00048         int errno;
00049         int result;
00050         int seek_pos;
00051         char buf[8];
00052 
00053 
00054         /* Check if the open files table entry indexing by fd is valid */
00055         if ( curthread->table_fd[fd] == NULL || fd >= MAX_FD)
00056         {
00057                 errno = EBADF;
00058                 return errno;
00059         }
00060 
00061         /* Get fcb using fd as index of open files table */
00062         fcb = curthread->table_fd[fd];
00063         
00064         /* Verify if is possible move seek pos for the file */
00065         result = VOP_TRYSEEK(fcb->vfs_node, pos);
00066         
00067         if ( result !=0 )
00068         {
00069                 errno = ESPIPE;
00070                 return errno;
00071         }
00072         
00073 
00074         /* Calculate new seek_pos */
00075         switch (whence) {
00076                 case SEEK_SET:
00077                         seek_pos = pos;
00078                         break;
00079                 case SEEK_CUR:
00080                         seek_pos = fcb->offset + pos;
00081                         break;
00082                 case SEEK_END:
00083                         u.uio_offset = 0;
00084                         u.uio_segflg = UIO_USERSPACE;
00085                         u.uio_rw = UIO_READ;
00086                         u.uio_space = curthread->t_addrspace;
00087                         do {
00088                                 fcb->offset = u.uio_offset;
00089                                 iov.iov_ubase = (userptr_t)buf; /* Location of the buffer */
00090                                 iov.iov_len = 1;
00091                                 u.uio_iov = &iov;
00092                                 u.uio_iovcnt = 1;
00093                                 u.uio_resid = 1;        
00094                                 u.uio_offset = fcb->offset;
00095 
00096                                 VOP_READ(fcb->vfs_node, &u);
00097                         } while (fcb->offset != u.uio_offset);
00098                         
00099                         seek_pos = fcb->offset + pos;
00100                         break;
00101                 default:
00102                         errno = EINVAL;
00103                         return errno;
00104         }
00105 
00106         /* Check seek_pos value */
00107         if( seek_pos < 0) {
00108                 errno = EINVAL;
00109                 return errno;
00110         }
00111 
00112         /* Update seek position */
00113         fcb->offset = seek_pos;
00114         
00115         /* Return the new seek position value */
00116         *retval = fcb->offset;
00117 
00118         return 0;
00119 
00120         
00121 }
